key_findings: Two user roles defined (Analyst, Leadership) with distinct view models — Leadership needs aggregate KPIs and SLO compliance; Analyst needs row-level filtering, graph drilldowns, and finding triage
Six views designed: Asset Discovery, Asset Detail, Finding Triage, Attack Surface Map (graph), HTTP Endpoint Browser, Operations Log, Executive Summary — all core journeys ≤ 5 clicks validated
Operations Log should be built first — immediately useful for 72h operation monitoring; lowest implementation complexity; highest immediate operational value
Attack Surface Map (graph) deferred to Phase 3 — highest complexity (requires graph rendering library); REST API table views sufficient for Phase 1-2 analyst intelligence consumption
URL-encoded filter state (shareable filter URLs) enables analyst collaboration via Mattermost — pre-filtered views shareable across team without screenshots
Graph view capped at 500 nodes (KRIL-prioritized display) — graph rendering with 10k+ nodes is unusable in browser; cap with visible count notice preserves most valuable attack surface view
AND-only filter combination chosen over OR — OR filters with 10+ dimensions create counterintuitive results and query performance issues; AND is the safe conservative default
Filter response time targets: ≤ 1s single-dimension, ≤ 3s complex multi-filter — requires Neo4j indexes on kril_score/severity/template_id and ClickHouse materialized views
Pivot map designed with max 3 hops from any entry point — analyst can navigate from finding → asset → graph without losing context or exceeding click budget
Interesting preset filters for HTTP Endpoint Browser: 'Juicy Endpoints' (200/401/403 + JSON), 'Admin Panels' (title contains admin), 'Dynamic Content' — accelerate analyst pattern recognition

decisions.title: Separate Analyst and Leadership roles with distinct view models
decisions.summary: Analyst: 5 operational views with row-level filtering. Leadership: 2 summary views with aggregate KPIs and export only.
decisions.rationale: A single view trying to serve both roles is cluttered for both. Leadership needs SLO compliance percentages; analysts need per-row drilldowns. Separate view models allow each role to operate without noise.

decisions.title: Operations Log built first
decisions.summary: Operations Log (Phase 1) → Asset Discovery + Asset Detail (Phase 1) → Finding Triage + Template Detail (Phase 2) → Graph View (Phase 3)
decisions.rationale: Operations Log delivers immediate value for 72h operation monitoring and requires only /api/v1/stats/ endpoints. Building it first provides visibility before analyst views are complete.

decisions.title: Graph view 500 node cap with KRIL prioritization
decisions.summary: Attack Surface Map displays max 500 nodes; if corpus exceeds, top 500 by KRIL score shown with visible count notice
decisions.rationale: Graph rendering with 10k+ nodes causes browser performance collapse. 500 nodes covers the most valuable attack surface. KRIL prioritization ensures highest-value nodes are visible.

decisions.title: URL-encoded shareable filter state
decisions.summary: Active filter state encoded in URL query parameters; links are shareable via Mattermost for analyst collaboration
decisions.rationale: Session-only filter state is not shareable. Analysts need to share pre-filtered views in Mattermost (e.g., 'look at these critical findings on this domain'). URL encoding is the standard mechanism.

decisions.title: AND-only filter combination
decisions.summary: All filters combined with AND semantics; no OR option in initial implementation
decisions.rationale: OR filters with 10+ dimensions create counterintuitive result sets and query planning complexity. AND is the analyst's natural filter intent ('show me assets that are KRIL > 70 AND resolved AND have findings').

decisions.title: Tech stack decision deferred to WO-00013
decisions.summary: Information architecture is technology-agnostic; all views defined as data models and user journeys
decisions.rationale: Frontend tech stack selection has distinct tradeoffs (hosting cost, dev familiarity, SSR vs CSR, etc.) that require separate analysis. Mixing stack decision into IA design creates coupling.

views.name: Asset Discovery
views.purpose: Primary workspace for identifying high-value targets
pivots: Asset Discovery filtered by domain (click root domain)

views.name: Asset Detail
views.purpose: Full detail for a single subdomain

views.name: Finding Triage
views.purpose: Prioritize and review vulnerability findings across full corpus

views.name: Template Detail
views.purpose: All assets affected by a specific vulnerability template

views.name: HTTP Endpoint Browser
views.purpose: Review discovered HTTP endpoints; identify interesting response patterns

views.name: Operations Log
views.purpose: Live operational monitoring of scan throughput, SLO status, and alerts

views.name: Executive Summary
views.purpose: One-page operation health for leadership; aggregate KPIs; no drilldown

user_journeys.journey: Find critical findings on high-KRIL assets
Identify all assets resolving to a specific IP
Find all assets vulnerable to a specific CVE
Export critical findings CSV for bug bounty submission
Explore attack surface neighbors of a target

build_phases.rationale: Highest complexity; requires graph rendering library

risk_model.title: Graph view unusable at high node count
risk_model.mitigation: 500 node cap; KRIL-prioritized display; visible count notice

risk_model.title: Filter query latency with complex multi-filter
risk_model.mitigation: Pre-indexed Neo4j properties; ClickHouse materialized views; ≤3s SLO

risk_model.title: Frontend over-complexity scope creep
risk_model.mitigation: Strict phase ordering; no graph view until Phase 3; no additions before Phase 1 ships

risk_model.title: Summary refresh delay > 60s
risk_model.mitigation: Operations Log backed by ClickHouse materialized view; 30s auto-refresh

recommended_actions.action: Build Operations Log first — immediately useful for 72h operation monitoring
Implement shareable filter URLs from day 1 — enables analyst collaboration via Mattermost
Add interesting preset filters to HTTP Endpoint Browser (Juicy Endpoints, Admin Panels, Dynamic Content)
Enforce 500 node cap in graph view with KRIL-based prioritization and visible count notice
Add Export CSV to Finding Triage and Template Detail views for bug bounty submission workflow
Implement persistent filter state within session with one-click Clear All Filters
Defer graph visualization to Phase 3; REST API table views sufficient for Phase 1-2
Tech stack decision delegated to WO-00013; this IA design is technology-agnostic

assumptions: A1: REST API endpoints are available as the data backend for all views
A2: Neo4j indexes on kril_score, severity, template_id are applied before frontend query load
A3: ClickHouse materialized views provide aggregate stats without full-table scans
A4: Frontend tech stack TBD in WO-00013; this design is framework-agnostic
A5: Authentication and role-based access control deferred to v1.1 (internal network only for v1)
A6: Mattermost available for analyst collaboration via shareable filter URLs
